response.justification: Maria Rodriguez brings 15+ years of solid full-stack engineering experience, open-source leadership, and some mentoring activity, which are genuine strengths. However, the role has a hard requirement of C1 German proficiency, and the candidate explicitly lists German as 'Basic' — this alone disqualifies her for a client-facing consulting leadership role in Germany. Beyond the language gap, the resume shows a strong individual-contributor trajectory with limited evidence of formal team leadership, IT architecture ownership, project management, UML, or IT security/risk management responsibilities, all of which are core to this Head of Development position.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse, hands-on software development experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Demonstrated open-source commitment and community engagement (GISCollective, conference talks, public workshops, multiple published libraries)
Comfort with Linux, modern DevOps tooling (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD), and polyglot development — aligns with the open-source and Linux emphasis in the JD
concerns: German listed as 'Basic' — role requires C1 minimum; this is a hard blocker for client advisory work in a German-market IT consultancy
No evidence of formal team leadership, headcount management, or structured technical project management — the role requires operational oversight of all active client projects
No mention of IT architecture methodologies, UML, requirements management, security/risk frameworks, or compliance experience — these are explicitly required qualifications

key_factors.factor: Breadth of open-source and technical experience
